retrieval_text: Poplar is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 171 works and 494 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Poplar, Poplars. Common plant-part co-mentions include Bark, Root, Leaf, Berry, Flower, Seed. Common preparation co-mentions include Tea, Powder, Syrup, Oil, Tincture, Decoction. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Fever, Cough, Dysentery, Rheumatism, Wounds or ulcers. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Child-use caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Toxicity watch, Bleeding caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include An improved system of botanic medicine : founded upon correct physiological principles : comprising a complete treatise on the practice of medicine with a copious materia medica; An improved system of botanic medicine : founded upon correct physiological principles : comprising a complete treatise on the practice of medicine with a copious materia medica / by Horton Howard.; An improved system of botanic medicine: founded upon correct physiological principles : embracing a concise view of anatomy and physiology : together with an illustration of the new theory of medicine : to which is added, a treatise on female complaints, midwifery, and the diseases of children (Volumes 1-2); An improved system of domestic medicine : founded upon correct physiological principles : comprising a complete treatise on anatomy and physiology, the practice of medicine, with a copious materia medica , and an extensive treatise on midwifery, embellished with over one hundred useful engravings, gotten up expressly for family use. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
